title: teucrium oxylepis subsp. marianum
intro: Teucrium oxylepis subsp. marianum, also known as the "Marian's Germander", is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, native to the Mediterranean region. This herb is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which contribute to its potential health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving respiratory issues. The Marian's Germander can be prepared as an herbal remedy in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and digestive problems. Additionally, its leaves and stems are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, making it a popular ingredient in herbal supplements and traditional remedies.
